# redis_set (Resource)

Manages a Redis set (`SADD`).

Changing `key` destroys and recreates the resource. On update, members removed from the configuration are deleted with `SREM`; new members are added with `SADD`. Member order is not preserved — Redis sets are unordered.

On `Create`, the provider issues a `DEL` on the target key before writing. This guarantees Terraform fully owns the key's contents. To adopt a key that already exists in Redis without losing its current members, use `terraform import` instead.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "redis_set" "example" {
  key     = "article:1:tags"
  members = ["terraform", "redis", "infrastructure"]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->

## Schema

### Required

- `key` (String) The Redis key.
- `members` (Set of String) The set of string members to store. Must contain at least one entry.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The Redis key, used as the resource identifier.

## Import

Any existing Redis set key can be imported using the key name. All current members are imported into state.

```shell
terraform import redis_set.example "article:1:tags"
```
